ops.text:
RESULTS (E21 complete; $0 spend, pure compute; all re-derivable via scripts/analyze.py over the read-only snapshot).

HEADLINE: sessions=813, pairs=6731, max_pairs_per_session=291, effective_sample_size=4019 (default within-session char-5-gram J>=0.8 weighting; reduction 0.597 of nominal 6731; session-block bootstrap 95% CI on reduction [0.586, 0.610] -> ESS CI [3944, 4104]), weights_path=/Users/yasyf/.cc-steer/experiments/E21/out/session-weights.parquet.

Distribution: mean 8.3, median 5, p90 17 pairs/session; Gini 0.57; 151 singleton sessions; top session 291 pairs (4.3% of all data from one session), top-10 sessions hold ~19% of pairs.

Two-axis redundancy finding (the key result):
- WITHIN-session context redundancy (char-5-gram on draft_text = pipeline's own metric): near-dups are 96.9% within-session at J0.8 (4281 within / 136 across of 4417 pairs). The pipeline's GLOBAL near_dup_representatives collapses 6731->3977 and only 21 of its 1516 multi-member clusters span >1 session -> the existing global dedup is already ~equivalent to session-aware dedup; it is NOT harmfully over-collapsing independent cross-session data. Effective independent pairs on this axis ~= 4019 (J0.8) / 2989 (J0.6) / 2641 (J0.5).
- CROSS-session correction redundancy (voyage-4-large cosine on the steer text, 2977 embedded positives): bimodal cosine (median 0.42, but ~13% of pairs at cos~1.0). Of near-identical (cos>=0.90) pairs, 99.2% are CROSS-session (580973 across / 4567 within). Same correction phrasing recurs across many distinct sessions. This is genuine REINFORCEMENT signal, not within-session double-counting, so it is deliberately OUT OF SCOPE for the session weights (correctly left un-deflated). Flag for a possible follow-up if E28 wants correction-level (not session-level) de-duplication.

ESS by scheme (reduction vs nominal 6731): ngram0.8=0.597 (DEFAULT), ngram0.6=0.444, ngram0.5=0.392, cos0.95=0.742, cos0.90=0.716, cos0.85=0.700, session_uniform(whole-session extreme)=0.121 (ESS=813). Kish ESS for default=5178.

WEIGHTS ARTIFACT out/session-weights.parquet (6731 rows, id unique, keyed by watcher `id` = canonical pair id; sft ids == watcher positives exactly, dpo/kto largely covered). Columns: id, session_id, label, category, cluster_size, weight(=1/within-session cluster size, default=ngram0.8), plus alt weight cols w_ngram08/06/05, w_cos095/090/085, w_session_uniform, and sizes sz_ngram08/sz_cos090. Invariant verified: sum(weight)=4019=ESS_sum=n within-session clusters; 4214 rows sit in a within-session near-dup cluster (weight<1). Rows lacking an embedding (all 3313 negatives + 441 positives) get weight 1.0 in the cos_* columns (treated as singletons).

Raw artifacts (verify-lane re-derivation): out/report.json (all metrics), out/pairs_per_session.csv, out/global_near_dup_pairs_j08.csv (every J>=0.8 near-dup pair with same_session flag), scripts/analyze.py. Methods reuse cc_steer.retrain.data.{shingles,jaccard,_minhash_signatures,_lsh_candidates,near_dup_representatives} for pipeline fidelity; within-session clustering is EXACT pairwise Jaccard (132147 within-session pairs enumerated, no LSH approximation); global within/across breakdown uses LSH (recall ~1.0 at J0.8, ~0.99 at J0.6, ~0.87 at J0.5 — cross-session counts at J0.5 are a slight undercount, noted).

PENDING-E15: point estimate + bootstrap CI reported for the ESS reduction; no promote/kill verdict (measurement feeding E28).
